sentiment: The systems we trust with memory are the ones most likely to forget us.

stories.title: Jurassic Park computers in excruciating detail
stories.summary: A meticulous archaeology of the Silicon Graphics machines that rendered a digital dinosaur park — now extinct hardware preserved only in screenshots and obsessive documentation.

stories.title: I tricked Claude into leaking your deepest, darkest secrets
stories.summary: An exploration of how AI memory systems can be manipulated into revealing private information — the very feature designed to help becomes the vulnerability.

stories.title: TS-2026-009: Insecure argument handling in Tailscale SSH permitted root access
stories.summary: A security bulletin in the tool millions trust to keep their networks safe — a reminder that the infrastructure of trust itself needs constant vigilance.
